name: Marbles
description: Random sampler with master clock. One t-section emits probabilistic gates, one x-section emits quantized random voltages. A deja-vu knob locks patterns and a scale learner stores up to six custom scales.

controls.name: Rate (t)
controls.description: Master clock rate. Drives the three T1/T2/T3 gate outputs. Overridden by external clock when T CLOCK patched.
controls.detail: ~0.5 Hz to ~100 Hz · bipolar attenuverter for T RATE CV

controls.name: Bias (t)
controls.description: Sets gate distribution between T1 and T3. CCW = only T1, CW = only T3, noon = balanced. T2 always fires as coin-flip between them.

controls.name: Jitter (t)
controls.description: Random clock jitter / tempo instability. CCW = locked grid, CW = progressively more timing drift up to fully random.

controls.name: t Mode
controls.description: Cycles Bernoulli (coin flip on each tick) · clusters (sparse/dense bursts) · divider (harmonic-ratio division of master clock).
controls.detail: Three LEDs indicate active mode · affects how T1/T2/T3 interrelate

controls.name: Deja Vu
controls.description: Pattern-locking probability. CCW = always fresh random. Noon = 50% probability a past step repeats. CW = full loop of length set by LENGTH.
controls.detail: Shared t & x by default · separate sections via t/x Deja Vu buttons

controls.name: Length (Deja Vu)
controls.description: Loop length when Deja Vu is active. 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16 steps selectable.
controls.detail: LED row shows current length · hold Length button + turn knob to edit

controls.name: Rate (x)
controls.description: X sample rate. Sets how often X1/X2/X3 redraw new voltages. Normalized to t clock but can be set independently.
controls.detail: External X CLOCK overrides · bipolar CV attenuverter

controls.name: Spread (x)
controls.description: Width of the random distribution around center. CCW = narrow/centered, CW = wide.

controls.name: Bias (x)
controls.description: Shifts center of the random distribution. Moves the average voltage up or down.

controls.name: Steps (x)
controls.description: Discrete-step count / quantization of the x output. Interacts with scale learner. Noon = continuous.
controls.detail: CW adds steps up to full scale quantization · CCW smooths toward continuous

controls.name: x Mode
controls.description: Cycles identical-independent-independent · 3 voices of 1 octave · shift-register (voltages chain down X1→X2→X3).
controls.detail: Shift-register mode produces melodic-in-motion relationships between voices

controls.name: t Deja Vu / x Deja Vu buttons
controls.description: Independent enable/disable of Deja Vu for the t and x sections. Lets timing loop while CV stays free, or vice-versa.

inputs.name: T CLOCK
inputs.description: External clock for t section. Replaces internal oscillator.

inputs.name: X CLOCK
inputs.description: External clock for x section. Falls back to T CLOCK (or internal if none).

inputs.name: X STEPS CV
inputs.description: CV for step count / quantization depth.

inputs.name: DEJA VU CV
inputs.description: CV for deja-vu probability (global for t and x).

outputs.name: T1 / T2 / T3
outputs.description: Three gate outputs. Distribution depends on t mode and bias. T2 is always a coin-flip between T1 and T3.

outputs.name: X1 / X2 / X3
outputs.description: Three random CV outputs. Range, spread, and quantization set by x controls.

outputs.name: Y
outputs.description: Fourth random CV output. Slow noise-like voltage, 16× slower than X by default, quantized by the same scale.

behaviors.name: t Bernoulli mode
behaviors.description: Each tick of the master clock independently chooses T1 or T3 based on bias. T2 fires on every clock tick. Classic probabilistic rhythm generator.

behaviors.name: t Clusters mode
behaviors.description: Gates emitted in bursts (dense groups) separated by silences. Bias sets cluster density; jitter adds timing variance. Good for Berlin-school and generative rhythmic bursts.

behaviors.name: t Divider mode
behaviors.description: T1/T2/T3 output harmonic ratios of master clock — bias selects the ratio. Works like a polyrhythmic clock divider with smooth bias-sweepable cross-fading.

behaviors.name: x Shift-register mode
behaviors.description: X1, X2, X3 behave as a 3-stage shift register: each clock shifts X1→X2, X2→X3; X1 draws a new value. Produces relationships where X2 and X3 are echoes of X1.

behaviors.name: Deja Vu locking
behaviors.description: At full CW the sequence loops exactly. Below CW, each step has a probability of being replaced by a new random value — so the loop slowly mutates. Can lock t and x independently via the sub-buttons.

behaviors.name: Custom scale learner
behaviors.description: Marbles learns up to six user scales from incoming V/OCT (press+hold scale button while playing notes). Slot 1 is chromatic, slots 2–6 are user-programmable.

behaviors.name: Y slow companion
behaviors.description: Y emits a random voltage that updates 16× slower than X by default, but shares the same scale quantization. Useful for slow transposition offsets or timbral drift alongside a fast melody on X.

behaviors.name: Internal vs external clocking
behaviors.description: Patching T CLOCK replaces the internal oscillator. The internal oscillator is high-quality (sub-ms jitter) and syncable to modular-standard clocks.

behaviors.name: X-clock fallback to T
behaviors.description: With no X CLOCK cable, X updates at the same rate as T, so x-rate knob acts as a divider/multiplier relative to T. Patching X CLOCK lets x and t run at entirely independent rates.

behaviors.name: Deja Vu pattern mutation
behaviors.description: Between half-CW and full-CW the locked pattern has a non-zero chance of swapping one step per cycle. Produces the 'Music for Airports' slowly-evolving motif effect.

patchIdeas.name: Locked generative melody
patchIdeas.patch: Deja Vu full CW over an 8-step loop turns X1 into a repeatable in-key melody that can be unlocked by easing Deja Vu back.
steps: Hold the X SCALE button and play a major scale on an external keyboard/sequencer into the t input jack to teach user scale slot 2. Release the button.
Press X SCALE until the LED above slot 2 lights.
Turn the DEJA VU knob fully clockwise.
Press the LENGTH button and turn the LENGTH knob until 8 LEDs are lit.
Set STEPS (x) to ~2 o'clock so the output is fully quantized.
Set RATE (t) to noon (~2 Hz internal clock).
Patch Marbles X1 → VCO V/OCT.
Patch Marbles T1 → envelope TRIG, then envelope → VCA CV.
Patch VCO → VCA → output. Ease DEJA VU back to ~3 o'clock any time to mutate one note per cycle.
patchIdeas.signalOut: X1 — quantized pitch CV, 0–5V, stepping through the same 8-note phrase until DEJA VU is eased.
patchIdeas.hear: A repeating 8-note in-key melody that locks tight on full CW, then starts swapping one note per cycle as you roll DEJA VU back.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* Scale: user slot 2 (major)
* Deja Vu: full CW
* Length: 8

- Marbles (X1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Bernoulli drum split
patchIdeas.patch: Bernoulli t-mode with bias at noon splits kick/snare as coin flips while T2 keeps a steady hat.
steps: Press the t MODE button until the green LED is lit (Bernoulli).
Set BIAS (t) to noon (50/50 kick vs snare).
Set RATE (t) to ~1 o'clock (roughly 120 BPM-ish feel).
Set JITTER (t) fully CCW for a tight grid.
Patch a slow LFO (cycle ~20 s) → T JITTER CV, attenuverter at ~10 o'clock for subtle human feel.
Patch Marbles T1 → kick module TRIG.
Patch Marbles T3 → snare module TRIG.
Patch Marbles T2 → hi-hat TRIG (fires every clock tick).
Leave DEJA VU fully CCW for endless fresh patterns, or turn CW with LENGTH 8 to lock a groove.
patchIdeas.signalOut: T1, T2, T3 — three 0/+5 V gates. T1+T3 are the coin-flip split, T2 is steady.
patchIdeas.hear: Classic probabilistic drum pattern: hats on every beat, kick and snare trading hits like a rolled die, with a breathing feel from the LFO on jitter.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Bernoulli (green)
* t Bias: noon
* t Jitter: CCW

- LFO (Slow) >> Marbles (T JITTER CV)
- Marbles (T1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Marbles (T2) t> Hat (Trig)
- Marbles (T3) t> Snare (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Shift-register harmonized voices
patchIdeas.patch: X shift-register mode makes X2 and X3 delayed echoes of X1 — three VCOs voice moving close-harmony chords.
steps: Hold X SCALE and play a minor scale into the module to learn user slot 3; release to save.
Press X SCALE until slot 3 LED lights.
Press the x MODE button until the red LED is lit (shift register).
Set STEPS (x) fully CW for full quantization, SPREAD (x) to ~1 o'clock, BIAS (x) to noon.
Set RATE (x) to noon so x advances on every t tick (X CLOCK unpatched).
Patch Marbles X1 → lead VCO V/OCT.
Patch Marbles X2 → harmony VCO V/OCT (this is last tick's X1).
Patch Marbles X3 → bass VCO V/OCT, optionally through a −1 octave offset (this is two ticks ago).
Patch Marbles T2 → envelope TRIG; envelope → a mixer VCA receiving all three VCOs.
Turn DEJA VU to ~2 o'clock with LENGTH 4 for a short evolving chord progression.
patchIdeas.signalOut: X1/X2/X3 — three quantized pitch CVs, 0–5V, each one step behind the previous.
patchIdeas.hear: A three-voice rolling canon in minor: the bass plays what the harmony played a moment ago, which played what the lead just played. Moves like a constantly shifting chord.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* x Mode: Shift Register (red)
* Scale: minor (slot 3)
* Deja Vu: ~2 o'clock
* Length: 4

- Marbles (X1) p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X2) p> Harmony VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X3) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T2) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Mixer VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Slow Y transposition
patchIdeas.patch: X1 plays the melody while Y — updating 16× slower and sharing the scale — walks the whole phrase to new keys.
steps: Press X SCALE until a user scale slot (e.g. slot 2 major) is selected.
Set STEPS (x) fully CW (fully quantized).
Set SPREAD (x) to ~1 o'clock and BIAS (x) to noon for mid-register melody.
Set RATE (t) to noon (~2 Hz); leave T CLOCK and X CLOCK unpatched.
Patch Marbles X1 → precision adder IN 1.
Patch Marbles Y → precision adder IN 2. Y updates ~16× slower than X1.
Patch adder OUT → melody VCO V/OCT.
Patch Marbles T1 → envelope TRIG; envelope → VCA CV; VCO → VCA → output.
Optional: turn DEJA VU CW with LENGTH 8 so the local melody repeats while Y keeps drifting the key.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Adder OUT — summed pitch CV (X1 melody + Y transposition), 0–~10 V, feeding one V/OCT jack.
patchIdeas.hear: A fast quantized melody that stays the same shape but every 16 notes or so slides up or down into a new key — like a modulating music-box.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* Scale: user slot 2
* Steps (x): full CW

- Marbles (X1) p> Adder (In 1)
- Marbles (Y) p> Adder (In 2) // slow transposition
- Adder (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Envelope (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Clusters mode for Berlin rhythm
patchIdeas.patch: Clusters t-mode bursts gates in dense flurries separated by silence — a Berlin-school rhythm engine.
steps: Press the t MODE button until the orange LED is lit (clusters).
Set BIAS (t) to ~2 o'clock for denser bursts.
Set JITTER (t) fully CCW for tight cluster timing.
Set RATE (t) to ~1 o'clock.
Patch Marbles T1 → kick TRIG.
Patch Marbles T3 → tom TRIG (fills).
Patch Marbles T2 → external sequencer CLOCK IN (sequencer advances one step per cluster tick).
Optional: DEJA VU ~3 o'clock + LENGTH 8 to loop a favorite flurry.
patchIdeas.signalOut: T1, T2, T3 — three 0/+5 V gate streams that fire in correlated bursts instead of evenly.
patchIdeas.hear: Rhythmic flurries: three or four fast hits, a breath of silence, then another burst — the classic gurgling Berlin-school cluster groove.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Clusters (orange)
* t Bias: ~2 o'clock
* t Jitter: CCW

- Marbles (T1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Marbles (T2) c> Sequencer (Clock)
- Marbles (T3) t> Tom (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized random bass + lead
patchIdeas.patch: X1 as a low-register bass and X3 as a high-register lead, each with its own gate, for an independent two-voice ostinato.
steps: Hold X SCALE and teach Marbles a pentatonic scale into slot 4; release.
Press X SCALE until slot 4 LED lights.
Press x MODE until the green LED is lit (independent — three separate random streams).
Set STEPS (x) fully CW (fully quantized).
Set BIAS (x) CCW (~9 o'clock) to shift the distribution low, SPREAD (x) ~1 o'clock.
Set DEJA VU to ~3 o'clock with LENGTH 8 for a mutating ostinato.
Patch Marbles X1 → bass VCO V/OCT (its low BIAS centre = bass range).
Patch Marbles X3 → lead VCO V/OCT through a +2 oct offset (for clearer lead register).
Patch Marbles T1 → bass envelope TRIG; T3 → lead envelope TRIG.
Feed each VCO through its own VCA into the mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: X1 → bass pitch CV (0–5 V, low bias); X3 → lead pitch CV (0–5 V, shifted up externally). T1/T3 are the matching 0/+5 V gates.
patchIdeas.hear: A slowly-mutating two-part ostinato — pentatonic bass notes below, sparser lead on top — where each voice keeps its own rhythm thanks to T1 and T3 being uncorrelated.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* Scale: user slot 4 (pentatonic)
* x Mode: Independent (green)
* Deja Vu: ~3 o'clock
* Length: 8

- Marbles (X1) p> Bass VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (X3) p> +2oct Offset p> Lead VCO (V/Oct)
- Marbles (T1) t> Bass Env (Trig)
- Marbles (T3) t> Lead Env (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Divider-mode polyrhythm clock
patchIdeas.patch: Divider t-mode turns T1/T2/T3 into three related clock divisions of an external master — a bias-sweepable polyrhythm engine.
steps: Press the t MODE button until the red LED is lit (divider).
Set JITTER (t) fully CCW (zero) for pristine timing.
Patch your master clock → Marbles T CLOCK. The internal oscillator is now overridden.
Set BIAS (t) to noon to start; turn it to pick different ratio sets (e.g. 3:4:6 vs 4:5:7).
Patch Marbles T1 → Sequencer 1 CLOCK IN.
Patch Marbles T2 → Sequencer 2 CLOCK IN.
Patch Marbles T3 → Sequencer 3 CLOCK IN.
Optional: patch a slow LFO → T BIAS CV (attenuverter ~10 o'clock) to morph ratios over time.
patchIdeas.signalOut: T1/T2/T3 — three phase-locked 0/+5 V clock streams at different harmonic divisions of T CLOCK. Mult each with a stackable if you need more destinations.
patchIdeas.hear: Three sequencers stepping at related but different speeds — the classic interlocking polyrhythm feel where phrases line up every few bars.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* t Mode: Divider (red)
* t Jitter: 0

- Master Clock (Out) c> Marbles (T CLOCK)
- Marbles (T1) c> Sequencer 1 (Clock)
- Marbles (T2) c> Sequencer 2 (Clock)
- Marbles (T3) c> Sequencer 3 (Clock)

patchIdeas.name: External clock, independent x rate
patchIdeas.patch: Feeding separate clocks to T CLOCK and X CLOCK lets you run fast arpeggios over a slow drum rhythm.
steps: Patch your main modular clock → Marbles T CLOCK.
Patch a fast clock (e.g. 8× the main rate, from a clock divider's ×8 output) → Marbles X CLOCK. This breaks the X-from-T normalization.
Press X SCALE until a user scale is lit, and set STEPS (x) fully CW for full quantization.
Set SPREAD (x) to ~1 o'clock and BIAS (x) to noon for mid-register.
Press t MODE until green (Bernoulli); BIAS (t) at noon.
Patch Marbles T1 → kick TRIG and T3 → snare TRIG for a slow beat.
Patch Marbles X1 → arp VCO V/OCT.
Patch Marbles T2 would re-fire on the slow grid — instead, mult the fast X CLOCK into the arp VCA envelope TRIG so each arp note gets an envelope.
DEJA VU ~3 o'clock, LENGTH 16 to loop the arp while the drums stay free.
patchIdeas.signalOut: X1 — fast quantized pitch CV at 8× the t rate, 0–5 V. T1/T3 are the slow 0/+5 V drum gates.
patchIdeas.hear: A slow Bernoulli kick/snare groove underneath a rapid 8-notes-per-beat in-key arpeggio — the two layers are clock-locked but sit at completely different densities.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Marbles:
* x Mode: Independent
* Steps (x): full CW

- Master Clock (Out) c> Marbles (T CLOCK)
- Fast Clock (Out) c> Marbles (X CLOCK)
- Marbles (T1) t> Kick (Trig)
- Marbles (T3) t> Snare (Trig)
- Fast Clock (Out) c> Arp Env (Trig) // stackable/mult
- Marbles (X1) p> Arp VCO (V/Oct)
